subject: A weathered animal skull (wolf or large predator) mounted as a totem on dark crossed wooden poles, with tattered feathers and cords dangling from it — the war banner of the Temur Frontier clan
description: A bleached predator skull, fanged jaws agape, sits lashed atop a structure of dark crossed wooden poles or spear-shafts. Long ragged feathers, cords, and strips of hide hang from the crossbeams, swaying as if in a cold wind. The banner is shown from a low angle against a pale, washed-out sky with hints of mountainous or tundra landscape behind it. The palette is muted — bone-white, weathered brown, ash-grey, and faded sky-blue. There are no figures present; the banner itself is the subject, a totemic standard rather than a wielded object.
foreground: animal skull totem mounted on crossed wooden poles with hanging feathers and cords
background: pale washed-out sky with faint suggestions of distant mountainous terrain
emotion: grim resolve, weathered defiance — a memento of survival
